digest.component_or_area: store-level compatibility record + fail-closed capability gate
propositions: Introduce a committed store-level compatibility record (format_version + required_capabilities) at a fixed path, written atomically and read by a fail-closed gate before any mutating or externally-publishing operation.

A v1.0 binary must fail closed on an unknown format_version or a required_capability not in KNOWN_CAPABILITIES, and on a corrupt/unreadable record, while treating an absent record as compatible legacy pass-through.

The gate is inserted at the single structural choke point inside lock.acquire() covering all lock-based mutations, plus explicit gates in fsck_recover_cli and reconcile._apply_mutations() for the no-lock raw-git and outbound Jira publish paths.
run_ensures must re-raise StoreIncompatibleError before its broad except Exception so the fail-closed gate is not silently swallowed on the ensure-sweep self-heal path.

Read-only commands (fsck diagnostic, show, list, search) stay available (exit 0) and surface the incompatibility via a machine-checkable compat_error object from a non-raising describe_store_compat(), while fsck repair mode is gated.
Rollback is safe because a pre-v1.0 binary ignores the unknown .store-compat.json file; scope excludes building an ordered migration ledger.
